tool.name: codcopy
tool.summary: Normalize coding-sequence or codon-profile input into a reusable codon profile

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `codcopy` normalizes a coding-sequence input or an existing normalized codon-profile input into the governed codon-usage profile model. It can also write that profile back out as a tab-separated reusable profile file for later `cai` or `codcmp` runs.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current v1 interface accepts one local source input and an optional `--profile-out` path. The source may be either a strict coding-sequence file or an existing normalized profile TSV with the governed codon-profile header.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The result is a normalized codon-usage profile. When `--profile-out` is supplied, the profile is written as tab-separated text with the stable header `codon\tamino_acid\tcount\tfrequency\tweight` so it can be reused directly by `cai` and `codcmp`.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema codcopy`. Rust service coverage writes a temporary codon-profile TSV from a committed coding fixture and then proves downstream interoperability by reusing that profile successfully in a `cai` invocation.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release is local-only and strict. Coding inputs are validated in the same way as the rest of the codon-analysis cohort, and profile inputs must use the governed TSV header and five-column row format exactly. This method does not attempt format inference beyond those two supported source shapes.

artifacts.label: Codon-reference FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed coding-reference FASTA fixture used to validate normalized profile generation.

examples.title: Normalize a coding fixture into a reusable codon-profile TSV
examples.description: Loads the committed coding-reference FASTA fixture, derives the governed codon-usage profile, and writes it as reusable TSV output for later `cai` or `codcmp` runs.

expected_outputs.label: Normalized codon-profile TSV
expected_outputs.description: A governed codon-profile TSV with the stable five-column header and weights suitable for later `cai` or `codcmp` input.
